steps.title: Compare the two texts
steps.content: Text 1 presents the consensus that curiosity is noninstrumental, a drive to acquire information for its own sake. Text 2 shows that when scientific information was framed as useful for humans, participants became more curious about it.

steps.title: Reject misread responses
steps.content: Choice B says people seek useless information because they expect later use, which Text 2 does not claim. Choice C raises a measurement problem not discussed. Choice D rejects curiosity as an explanation for purposeless information seeking, but Patel and colleagues acknowledge information acquisition as a motivator. The answer is A.
